action.reasoning: Observation: Inspected workspace files via command output (train.py, predict.py, model.py with SchNet+PyG on 3D QM9 data). Prior iter2 segfault (exit 139) during training, no eval score yet. Analysis: Likely root cause is DataLoader multiprocessing (num_workers=4/2) conflicting with PyG batching/CUDA init on this env; common PyG issue. Other code looks correct (mol_to_data, SchNet forward with z/pos/batch, L1Loss, metrics.jsonl logging, ckpt saving). Decision: Update model.py (fix import), train.py/predict.py (num_workers=0, batch_size=16 for stability/speed, epochs=5 on 20k subsample), run training for baseline, submit_eval immediately to get MAE score + measure full iter time before longer runs.
